stem: A web performance study measures the response time (ms) for a sample of 40 website users. The sample mean is 113.0 ms with a sample standard deviation of 31.2 ms. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 113.0 ms. 95% CI = [103.0, 123.0]. Point estimate: 113.0 ms.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
